Marginal Single-Variant Association Scan

Source: R/marginal_scan.R
marginal_scan.Rd

Performs genome-wide marginal association analysis from a GDS file with chunked processing for memory efficiency. Supports standard asymptotic and saddlepoint approximation (SPA) methods.

marginal_scan(
  gds_file,
  null_model,
  sample_id = NULL,
  use_SPA = NULL,
  chunk_size = 2000L,
  mac_cutoff = 1L,
  missing_imputation = "mean",
  output_csv = NULL,
  verbose = 1
)

Arguments

gds_file

Character path to GDS file.

null_model

A glow_null_model object from fit_null_model().

sample_id

Optional character vector of sample IDs to override null_model$sample_id for GDS sample alignment. Default: NULL (uses null_model$sample_id).

use_SPA

SPA control: NULL (auto: TRUE for binary, FALSE for continuous), TRUE (force SPA, binary only), FALSE (standard only), or "both" (equivalent to TRUE). Default: NULL.

chunk_size

Integer number of variants per processing chunk. Default: 2000L.

mac_cutoff

Integer minimum minor allele count. Variants with MAC < mac_cutoff are excluded from results. Default: 1L.

missing_imputation

Character method for missing genotypes: "mean" or "zero". Default: "mean".

output_csv

Optional path to write results as CSV. Default: NULL.

verbose

Integer verbosity level (0=silent, 1=progress, 2=detailed).

Value

A data.frame with class "glow_marginal_scan" containing columns: chr, pos, ref, alt, variant_id, MAF, MAC, score, var_score, Z, pvalue. When SPA is active, additional columns Z_SPA and pvalue_SPA are included.

Details

For each chunk of variants, genotype dosages are read from the GDS file using SeqArray, missing values imputed (mean or zero), and marginal score statistics computed from the pre-fitted null model.

Score statistic: \(S_j = G_j^T (Y - \mu_0)\) where \(\mu_0\) are fitted values under the null. Variance is computed using the hat-matrix projection to account for covariates. The Z-statistic is \(Z_j = S_j / \sqrt{Var(S_j)}\).

When SPA is enabled (recommended for binary traits with case-control imbalance), SPAtest::ScoreTest_SPA() is called per chunk and SPA-corrected p-values are stored in the pvalue_SPA column.

Complexity: O(n * m) per chunk where n is sample count and m is chunk_size. Memory per chunk is O(n * chunk_size). Total runtime is linear in the number of variants.

References

Dey, R., Schmidt, E. M., Abecasis, G. R., and Lee, S. (2017). A fast and accurate algorithm to test for binary phenotypes and its application to PheWAS. American Journal of Human Genetics, 101(1), 37-49. doi:10.1016/j.ajhg.2017.05.014

See also

extract_pheno_covar_gds, fit_null_model, getZ_marg_score, annotate_gds_marginal

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
pheno <- extract_pheno_covar_gds("chr22.gds", covar_names = "sex")
nm    <- fit_null_model(pheno$X, pheno$Y, trait = "binary",
                        sample_id = pheno$sample_id)
results <- marginal_scan("chr22.gds", nm, use_SPA = FALSE, verbose = 1)
head(results)
} # }

Contents
